channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump afirma que los soldados caídos apoyaban la guerra con Irán
item.description: El presidente Donald Trump no dijo cómo conocía las opiniones de los soldados caídos, pero decidió hablar por ellos en un momento político peligroso, en el que los estadounidenses se han mostrado cada vez más críticos respecto a la guerra en Irán.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Donald J. Trump salutes during a dignified transfer for the remains of U.S. Army soldiers killed in Jordan, in Dover, Del., on Wednesday.

item.title: La apuesta de Putin podría dar resultados
item.description: A pesar de todo el peligro de la guerra, Putin ha apostado por aguantar más que Occidente.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Notte

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración de The New York Times; foto de consorcio por Maxim Shipenkov

item.title: La primera ministra de Japón dice que solo duerme de ‘0 a 3 horas’
item.description: Una reciente publicación en las redes sociales de Sanae Takaichi ha provocado un debate en el país sobre el equilibrio entre la vida laboral y personal.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno

media:content.media:description: La primera ministra Sanae Takaichi en una reunión en el Parlamento de Japón en Tokio la semana pasada. Una publicación de Takaichi en redes sociales sobre su horario ha provocado un debate sobre las horas de trabajo y los peligros de la privación del sueño.

item.title: Viajar con autismo: consejos y tácticas
item.description: Los expertos afirman que planificar con anticipación y adaptar las experiencias a las necesidades del viajero puede ser de gran ayuda.
item.dc:creator: Julie Weed

item.title: Repairing Storm Damage Is Expensive. It’s Only Going to Get Worse.
item.description: A report from a group called Rebuild by Design estimates that New York will have to spend more than half a trillion dollars to repair the effects of climate change in the next 24 years.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

media:content.media:credit: Lauren Lancaster for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A small bridge that was washed out in a flood three years ago has been rebuilt in Highland Falls, N.Y.

item.title: N.Y.C. Schools Miss Out on $431 Million in Medicaid Payouts, Audit Finds
item.description: The comptroller’s office said that the city’s Department of Education had lacked documentation or did not seek reimbursement for special education services.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag
item.category: Advocates for Children of New York

media:content.media:credit: Gabriele Holtermann-Gorden/SIPA, via Associated Press Images
media:content.media:description: New York City’s comptroller, Mark Levine, said it was “quite troubling” that the Department of Education had failed to seek Medicaid reimbursements.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 23, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: El futuro de una ciudad fronteriza depende de las próximas medidas comerciales de Trump
item.description: Laredo, Texas, se ha convertido en el principal puerto de entrada de EE. UU. Las nuevas negociaciones podrían echar por tierra ese éxito.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson

media:content.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times

item.title: Late Night Rags on Trump and His Cabinet Joining TikTok
item.description: “Right now TikTok is, like, ‘Oh, no, are we the new Facebook?’” Jimmy Fallon said on “The Tonight Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Global Oil Prices Rise as Conflict With Iran Deepens
item.description: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Laura Stevens
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Haiti Needs Stability, Not Mass Deportations
item.description: Deporting Haitian migrants will further destabilize a country in crisis.
item.dc:creator: Russel L. Honoré

media:content.media:credit: Naomieh Jovin for The New York Times

item.title: Quote of the Day: From Skeptical to Smitten: An American Evolution
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, July 23, 2026.

item.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
item.description: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: American and Jordanian soldiers participating in a military exercise northeast of Amman in 2022.

item.title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Fabeha Monir for The New York Times

item.title: Japan’s Leader Invokes Her Hero, Shinzo Abe, to Drive Nationalist Agenda
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is leveraging her ties to Mr. Abe, the slain Japanese leader, as she pushes to reshape defense and the economy.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

media:content.media:credit: Kentaro Takahashi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sanae Takaichi has frequently leveraged her ties to Shinzo Abe.

item.title: How to Eat a 2,000-Pound Fish? These Orcas Choose to Pulverize It.
item.description: Marine biologists in Mexico captured video of a strange maneuver that killer whales used to shred their meal.
item.dc:creator: Alexa Robles-Gil

item.title: Former Head of All-Girls School Indicted, Accused of Ignoring Abuse Reports
item.description: Jean Norris, who ran Miss Hall’s School in Massachusetts, was charged with reckless endangerment of a child. A former teacher under her tenure faces three rape charges.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:credit: David Degner for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Miss Hall’s School in Pittsfield, Mass.

item.title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
item.description: Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: Dr. Amish Shah has preached about the value of door-knocking.

item.title: Republican Lawmaker Urges Charges Against Jack Smith
item.description: The criminal referral could provide a catalyst for a prosecution of Mr. Smith, who as special counsel became a top target of the president’s retribution campaign.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush

media:content.media:description: Jack Smith, a former special counsel, testifying before the House Judiciary Committee in January.

item.title: Fire in San Diego County Prompts Evacuation Orders
item.description: More than 2,000 people have been told to flee the Creelman fire, which has burned more than 300 acres without any containment near Ramona, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker and Livia Albeck-Ripka

item.title: Man Found Guilty in 2023 Stabbings Near UC Davis, and Is Ruled Insane
item.description: A jury this week found Carlos Reales Dominguez guilty of involuntary manslaughter in two deaths, but a judge ruled that he was insane at the time of the crimes. He faces time in a mental hospital.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

media:content.media:description: Carlos Reales Dominguez during his arraignment in Yolo County Superior Court in Woodland, Calif., in 2023.

item.title: Debate Over Ancient Iranian Jewelry Worn by Zendaya to ‘Odyssey’ Event
item.description: The actress wore earrings to an event for “The Odyssey” that a London gallery has said were made from an ancient artifact.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

media:content.media:credit: Scott A Garfitt/Invision, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Zendaya at an event for “The Odyssey” in London this month.

item.title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
item.description: The Saudi authorities confirmed a strike on one of the ships. These are the first claimed attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A satellite image of the Bab al-Mandab Strait earlier this month.

item.title: Sony to Reopen Hollywood’s Famed Cinerama Dome
item.description: Sony Pictures Entertainment said on Wednesday that it would reopen the Cinerama Dome, a Hollywood landmark, in 2028 after a renovation.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna
item.category: Cinerama Dome (Los Angeles, Calif, Movie Theater)

item.title: Massachusetts House Passes Bill That Would Loosen Late-Term Abortion Rules
item.description: The bill would leave it to physicians to decide if an abortion is necessary after 24 weeks of pregnancy. Current law allows such abortions only under certain circumstances.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.category: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists

media:content.media:credit: Kylie Cooper for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Massachusetts House passed a bill to loosen the rules on late-term abortions. The Senate is expected to vote on the measure before the legislative session ends next week.

item.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
item.description: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and William J. Broad
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
Abdulaziz bin Salman, Prince of Saudi Arabia

media:content.media:description: President Trump with Mohammed bin Salman, the crown prince of Saudi Arabia, at the White House last year.

item.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
item.description: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany

media:content.media:description: President Trump disclosed in June that he and his family had made $1.4 billion in crypto revenue.

item.title: How the Pentagon Withholds Iran War Details
item.description: Our Pentagon correspondent Helene Cooper explains how a lack of transparency reflects a tension between the Pentagon’s claims of operational security and the American public’s right to know what the military is doing in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Paul Abowd, Jon Hazell, Whitney Shefte, Rafaela Balster and Stephanie Swart
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Mamdani Knew He Couldn’t Arrest Netanyahu. But He Could Denounce Him.
item.description: In a video message, the mayor of New York City said he could not legally arrest Israel’s prime minister but reinforced his position as a voice of the international left.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg, Dana Rubinstein and Liam Stack
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: New York City Mayor's Office, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani used a two-minute video address to criticize the Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, while acknowledging that he lacked the power to arrest him if he came to New York.

item.title: ‘Charlie Kirk Memorial Highway’ Signs Face Backlash From Vietnam Veterans
item.description: The signs appeared last month on a stretch of Tennessee State Route 386 north of Nashville that was designated Vietnam Veterans Boulevard nearly four decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Alena Cerro

item.title: OpenAI dice que sus modelos de IA se descontrolaron y atacaron una biblioteca digital
item.description: El ataque, dirigido a los sistemas informáticos de la empresa Hugging Face, ocurrió mientras OpenAI evaluaba sus propios sistemas.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger

media:content.media:credit: Aaron Wojack para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: OpenAI y otras empresas han advertido que sus nuevos sistemas de IA podrían convertirse en poderosas herramientas para los piratas informáticos.

item.title: Trump’s Plan for Science: More Money for A.I., Less for Universities
item.description: Michael Kratsios, President Trump’s science adviser, proposed overhauling how the government funds research. Democrats said Mr. Trump’s actions had weakened science.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Flavelle and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

media:content.media:description: Michael Kratsios, the director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, testified before a House committee on Wednesday.

item.title: Why the Student Opposition Movement Is Growing in India
item.description: Thousands of young people angry about India’s education system continued to protest in New Delhi on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:description: Activists and supporters of the Cockroach Janta Party clashed with police officers during a protest in New Delhi on Monday.

item.title: Peter Lassally, Longtime Late-Night TV Producer, Dies at 93
item.description: He was known as the “host whisperer” for his success at producing shows starring Johnny Carson, David Letterman and Craig Ferguson.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.category: Late Show With David Letterman (TV Program)
The Late Late Show with Craig Ferguson (TV Program)

media:content.media:credit: J. Emilio Flores for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Peter Lassally in an undated photo. One of his strengths was “getting the host to understand how to listen,” the comedian Garry Shandling said, and “how to ask the questions that an audience might want to hear.”

item.title: What Visuals Show About Recent Damage to U.S. Military Sites in the Middle East
item.description: Video footage and satellite images reveal new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets during the latest flare-up in fighting.
item.dc:creator: Aric Toler and Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Doreen Broadnax, Famous (as Sparky D) for ’80s Rap Battle, Dies at 61
item.description: Tossing verbal grenades back and forth with Roxanne Shanté, she helped establish a lasting hip-hop truism: Bad blood is good business.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

media:content.media:description: Doreen Broadnax in 1988, after the end of the so-called Roxanne Wars, one of the first rap battles.

item.title: The U.S. Signs a Nuclear Pact With Saudi Arabia
item.description: Also, Trump threatens to retaliate against civilian targets in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Prince Mohammed bin Salman and President Trump at the White House last year.

item.title: Jason Alexander Apologizes for 2012 Sketch With Teenage Courtney Stodden
item.description: Alexander rubs a phone against the actress’s breasts in the Funny or Die comedy sketch, which was published when she was 17.
item.dc:creator: Reggie Ugwu

media:content.media:description: “I completely agree that it was inappropriate and I truly regret it,” Jason Alexander said.

item.title: One-Pan Dumplings With Greens
item.description: This lifesaver of a recipe, linked below, calls for cooking dumplings and kale together in one skillet.

media:content.media:credit: Ghazalle Badiozamani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.

item.title: El juicio de Nicolás Maduro comenzaría en junio de 2027
item.description: Maduro y Cilia Flores, su esposa, fueron capturados por el ejército estadounidense en enero y desde entonces han estado recluidos en una cárcel de Brooklyn. Se han declarado no culpables de los cargos en su contra.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich

media:content.media:description: Un juez federal fijó la fecha de inicio del juicio de Nicolás Maduro para junio de 2027.

item.title: U.S. Signs Deal to Help Saudis Obtain Nuclear Power
item.description: The accord is intended to further bind the United States with the Saudi government as the war with Iran strains the relationship.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.category: Abdulaziz bin Salman, Prince of Saudi Arabia

media:content.media:description: President Trump with Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud, prime minister of Saudi Arabia, during an arrival ceremony at the Royal Palace in Riyadh, last year.

item.title: El nuevo póster de ‘La La Land’ corrige la mano de Ryan Gosling
item.description: Durante una escena de baile, la mano de Gosling no coincidía con la de Emma Stone, un detalle que lo persiguió durante años después del estreno.
item.dc:creator: Malia Mendez

media:content.media:description: Justin Manfredi, vicepresidente ejecutivo de Lionsgate, comentó en tono jocoso lo “radicalmente diferente” que era un póster revisado de La La Land respecto de su versión anterior.

item.title: They Wrote a Picture Book to Encourage Bipartisanship
item.description: Ellie Gottheimer and Larra Mullin, who met when their fathers were in Congress, make a case for bipartisanship in a picture book about dogs working together.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

media:content.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Inspired by their fathers’ friendship, Ellie Gottheimer, left, and Larra Mullin wrote “Shmoo & Ozzie Go to Washington.”

item.title: I’m Disabled. My Sex Life is Amazing.
item.description: The filmmaker and actor Kyla Harris talks about how an occupational therapist helped her find a way to use a sex toy with a partner. “As a disabled person, the thing that I rely on most is creativity,” she told Anna Martin, host of the “Modern Love” podcast.
item.dc:creator: The Modern Love Podcast Team

item.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Agents Amid Push for Private Security
item.description: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.
item.dc:creator: Ryley Ober

media:content.media:description: Tampa International is one of three U.S. airports opting in to a new program.

item.title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
item.description: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: In the days leading up to the vote, various House Republican factions had quietly voiced grievances about the plan.

item.title: The Next Big Tests for the Left: Michigan, Wisconsin and Minnesota
item.description: Progressives are now fighting in swingier terrain in purple or light-blue states.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood

item.title: House Republicans Pass Stock Trading Bill Tied to SAVE Act and Voter ID Measure
item.description: The measure has drawn bipartisan backing amid a public outcry against the practice, but Democrats opposed it after Republicans added a voter ID requirement.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz

media:content.media:description: People lining up to vote in Wilkes-Barre, Pa., in 2024. The stock trading measure faces a murky fate in the Senate after Republican leaders folded in voting restrictions favor by President Trump.

item.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Becomes a Police Station
item.description: The Austrian government has turned the building where Adolf Hitler was born into a police station in an attempt to deter the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers. The building opened on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: David Cheung
